channel.title: Posts on Jorge Branch's Tech Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Jorge Branch's Tech Blog

item.title: Shenandoah GC - An Introduction
item.description: Introduction Most modern languages, such as Java, C#, Python, Ruby, Go or JavaScript don’t require direct developer intervention in the management of memory. For the creation of a new Point object, a call to new Point() is enough, and all the particular details of how the respective memory is going to be allocated and eventually deallocated are left to the virtual machine’s garbage collector. Barring Rust and Objective-C, the trend over the last decades is evident: all new languages are garbage collected.
